title: Open Knowledge Format: Building Both Sides
summary: Google's Open Knowledge Format represents agent knowledge as linked markdown files instead of a vector store. I built both the producer and consumer to understand it hands-on — and found its retrieval mechanism is structurally the same lexical search that failed on paraphrased queries in an earlier benchmark.

title: Lexical vs Semantic Search - What the data actually shows
summary: I tested BM25-adjacent, vector, and hybrid retrieval on 10 real markdown files. Lexical hit@5 on semantic queries was 0.00. Vector didn't struggle on exact terms. And hybrid didn't always win. Here's what the data actually said.

title: Dynamic Model Routing in AI Agent Systems
summary: Not every query needs GPT-4. Four practical patterns for routing between models at runtime — keyword routing, cost-tier routing, tool-intent routing, and try-cheap-with-fallback. When to use each and what you trade off.
